summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that n8n uses @n8n/vm2 by default for Code node execution (N8N_RUNNERS_ENABLED defaults to false), and a known vm2 sandbox escape payload achieves code execution outside the sandbox, writing a proof file to disk.
expected_security_impact: A workflow containing a Code node with a vm2 escape payload should execute arbitrary code outside the sandbox, creating a proof file at /tmp/evohunt-vm2-escape-proof on the host filesystem.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched n8n with default configuration, created an owner account, created and executed a workflow with a Code node containing a vm2 escape payload, and the proof file was created, confirming the sandbox escape.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the existence and content of the proof file /tmp/evohunt-vm2-escape-proof after workflow execution. The file contains the output of a command that should only be reachable outside the vm2 sandbox, distinguishing a real escape from a sandbox-internal error.
evidence_produced_by_run: The proof file /tmp/evohunt-vm2-escape-proof was created with content EVOHUNT_VM2_ESCAPED, confirming code execution outside the vm2 sandbox.
The n8n server started with N8N_RUNNERS_ENABLED=false (the default), confirming VM2 is the default execution path.
The workflow execution completed without n8n reporting a sandbox violation, indicating the escape was transparent to the application.
limitations_or_blockers: Building n8n from source inside the Docker container requires significant time (approximately 5-15 minutes).

The specific vm2 escape payload may need to be adjusted for future versions of @n8n/vm2 if the maintainers patch individual escape vectors, though the vm2 architecture remains fundamentally vulnerable.
The escape payload used targets @n8n/vm2 3.9.25; different payloads may be needed for other versions.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the n8n repository from GitHub, checks out the pinned commit n8n@1.123.1, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
